name: Jack Benny
abstract: Jack Benny (born Benjamin Kubelsky; February 14, 1894 – December 26, 1974) was an American entertainer who evolved from a modest success as a violinist on the vaudeville circuit to one of the leading entertainers of the twentieth century with a highly popular comedic career in radio, television, and film. His radio and television program…

concerts.label: Benefit: Committee to Save Carnegie Hall and The National Association for Retarded Children

Introduction and Rondo Capriccioso in A Minor, Op. 28

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to Amos and Andy, and people like that, you know, coming up. And Mert and Marge, Bing Crosby, Jack Benny, people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: Walter O’Keefe to me was like a Fred Allen or a Jack Benny. Very funny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Cantor and Jack Benny and all those people changed their names.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: I think he was 39 then... I think I'm a little bit older than Jack Benny was.

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: my childhood acquaintances were Nat King Cole and Judy Garland and the Andrews Sisters and Sophie Tucker, Jack Benny. So without knowing who they were, I was being imbued with this culture of music
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: We're talking about comedians now. We wrote some names down. Who's that? Johnny Taylor was a comedian that used to work at Rochester before Rochester got with Jack Benny. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
